page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.6xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1891.59 and a multi-core score of 23333.96. Memory bandwidth tests show 1286.38 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 131.14 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 64.78 GB/sec for large-block writes. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference text generation for small 135M models, achieving 302.83 tokens per second, while medium 7B model text generation runs at an average speed of 15.35 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7t.6xlarge offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention. This hardware configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and software compilation workloads.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average range for general compute and memory bandwidth, though it exhibits strong performance in text generation for small language models. The dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density make this instance well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and software compilation tasks.
